tokens: You just stay back there and take your time.
I'll go out here and scout the area.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete and Al, your LM shadow should be about 110 feet.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'm looking for...I'm dying to find out what this mound is over here anyhow, Al.
(Pause) We got a very peculiar mound sticking up out of the ground, Houston.
I want to go look at it.
As a matter of fact, I think I'll go take a picture of it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Could you give me your position and distance with respect to the LM?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (To Gibson) Pete's about...I'd guess, about 300 feet (90 meters) at 12 o'clock (from the LM) in the bottom of a shallow crater that you're bound to see on your map.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm headed to the right-hand edge of the Head Crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Here's a neat spot to put it out up here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Is it flat for a good piece?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's a good long ways away too; it must be at least, what, 500 feet from the LM?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's the world's most peculiar...I got to photograph this thing.
I can't imagine what it is.
The mound's sticking up; and I can't imagine how it got there or what would make it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Probably commenting on Pete's running) Boy, you can cover the ground on this lightweight...at one-sixth g.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I got to get them a stereo of this thing.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, at 1 plus 48 into the EVA, you're looking good.
Looks as though you're right on there, if you've just about completed your traverse.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Now, look, Al, look over here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We're way out from the LM.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's a good...Hey, there's another one of those mounds over there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What do you suppose they are?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I don't know, Houston, what they are; they're just sort of mounds.

Looks like - don't take this the wrong way - it looks like a small volcano, only it's just about 4 feet high and, at the top, it's about 5 feet across; and it then slopes from the top on down to the level with the terrain, and that diameter - that circle where it finally becomes level with the terrain - is about 15 or 20 feet.
So it looks sort of like a small volcano.
There's a couple of them out here.
They look like they were formerly made out of mud or something.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Is there any hole or central vent?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I got to go over...We'll go over after we get the ALSEP out.
There's a couple of them here.
(Pause) This is a...We couldn't ask for a better spot to put this ALSEP down.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, (there are a) lot more rocks up here (than there are around the LM).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Houston) Listen, there...We could play geologist for two days and never get any further than we are right now.
Seeing all different kinds of things.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That handle, you know, when you carry this thing around in one g, the ALSEP tends to hang down.
But you carry it around up here at one-sixth and, particularly, the RTG tends to rotate the whole pallet.
So, in a few minutes, you've got one of them kind of half way up in the air and the other one's down by your left leg, being afraid that the handle's going to come undone.
You know, the handle doesn't lock.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What happened to all those...Hey, that's slick.
You know these neat little decals we got on here?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You can't hardly read them in the sunlight because they don't have enough contrast to them.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me make sure now that we're not going to run out into some holes.
Pete, I'm going to move just a little bit further to the east - or, correction - to the north, so that I won't end up over in that hole with the SIDE.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And I think it would be a real good spot.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And I think this is the spot, Pete, right here.
(Pause) Let me look and make sure, now, that we're going to have a good place for everything.
Magnetometer can sit over there and the seismometer will sit in a good flat place.
Although, the trouble with the seismometer (is that) we don't have any good solid bedrock or anything to set it on.
All we've got is this dirt.
And I don't see any area around that has any rock.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm afraid we're just going to have to take what we can get on this seismometer.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You know, there must be some thermal expansion or something.
I'm having a heck of a time getting this UHT (Universal Handling Tool) in this SIDE.
(Pause) It just flat won't go in there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, just pick it up with your hands.
I always handled the SIDE (Suprathermal Ion Detector Experiment) with my hands.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I can't bend down that far.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Ready to take the SIDE cable to the Central Station as per
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I had to do it, Pete, so that I'd have a good place for the (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm going to try to do that.
This ground, you know, it doesn't get hard as you move down a couple of inches.
If you tamp it, and that's what I'm planning to do...But I don't know.
We'll just have to see what happens.
I'm worried about it getting a thermal short myself.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Ridiculous (Laughs) I remember how they took care of this white paint.
You had to have gloves to touch it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I got a kind of a problem here.
(Pause) I'm going to have to do this myself.
I'll have to do this backwards; but it's going to work right.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Garbled) I'll be with you in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You can relieve me in this SIDE right here while I (garbled), because it looks like it might...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, but let me get it plugged in.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: They (the cables) sure splay around under this one-sixth g, don't they?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Should be (the) same as on that other one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Obviously, it's on the side I can't see.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Long Pause) You know, that noise - that whistling - is coming from you, Pete, because every time you move around, it whistles.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (We don't want to) drop that baby (the SIDE?).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me have the cable right there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) The next thing I got to do is head this way with it, right?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Before you do that, come here a second, would you, please?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, but let me set it down one moment.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You can close it, though, before it gets any dirt in it.
You can just reach up there and close it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Before we get any dirt on it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Never saw it do it before.
(Garbled) We could still salvage that, though.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now kind of turn it over with your other hand, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Where do you want to go with it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, I want to see the...Let me get this.
Now, let's see the other side.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah; this won't hurt a thing.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me just put this down here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, before you set the Central Station down for the final time, if you would also tamp that ground down, it would help in keeping the dirt off of the thermally-sensitive areas.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (To Al) Oh, I did it, I did it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Laughing) I set it (meaning the SIDE) down and it didn't fall over.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Use your tongs to hold this (probably ALSEP package 2 which is the base for the RTG) up a minute.
It (the RTG)'s a little hot, and I don't want to touch it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Right there in your...There you got them.
Now if you'll just hold it steady, then I can do the rest.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll go get the rest of the stuff over here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now we're going to get the easy part.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The name of the game is to get the ALSEP here.
(Running or, at least moving quickly) Whoooom!
Up through one crater and over another.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Does that look as good as it feels?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, I'll tell you the way to do it.
Tend to rock from side to side as you run.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You really move better that way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Here I come, ready or not.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We are really getting dirty out here.
There's no way to handle all this equipment with all the dust on it.
Every time you move something, the dust flies; and, in this low gravity, it really takes off.
Goes way up in the air and then comes down and lands on you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: How far do you estimate we're from the LM?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Coming after the (Central Station) antenna mast (which had been serving as the carry bar and was left attached to ALSEP Package 1 that includes the Central Station and holds various experiment packages).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me plug this thing (the RTG cable connector) in (the Central Station base), Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I can't get down low enough to get it (the carry bar) off.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll get it for you, if you can't.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me move that dust cover for you.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Responding to Al) Strikes me pretty good.
I'll be tamping the dirt around this Central Station.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) You're going to have to design things with little legs on them or something, so you can put them down and they stand off the soil.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: If we had that suit we could bend over in, we'd have the job done by now.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, can you push down on this side of that?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: My biggest sweat is trying to bend over.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: No, let me look at it a second.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Which way do you want this?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) I had to really push that other one (that is, the SIDE cable connector).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get it with you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now we won't touch the shorting button.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We've connected the RTG to the Central Station, Houston.
And we're ready to go to work deploying the experiments.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, did you experience any trouble with the dust cover on the SIDE?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: It opened, Houston, and we closed it again, and we got no dirt on it, fortunately.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me tamp the dirt down underneath the Central Station.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, I started to walk away with the Central Station attached to the UHT via the little gizzy.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Have I got it tamped pretty good?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Need a little bit more this way, I think.
The problem with tamping, Pete, it looks like every time you tamp that, as your foot comes up, you know it...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Get it right down the Sun mark.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I've got to get the solar wind, huh?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Houston, how we doing on the timeline?
(No answer; Long Pause) Do you read me.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: How we doing on the timeline?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: They may be talking to Yankee Clipper.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, did you have a reading on the shorting amps?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Yeah; I was asking you how we were doing on the timeline?
(No answer; Long Pause) Can't see that (shorting amp) needle anywhere.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete and Al, at 2 hours and 7 minutes into the EVA, you're about 5 minutes behind.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I don't even see a needle in there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's not reading zero, and I don't see a needle.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I don't even see a needle in there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, did you get our last comment?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We can't see a needle in the shorting amps (meter) anywhere.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What I've done for the seismometer, Houston, is I've sort of dug a little crater so that, essentially, the hole in the center of the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Gibson) We'll see how it works.
It looks like it might work.
I tamped it also in the small crater there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I've got the solar wind deployed here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Boy, you really have to be careful of these cables, don't you?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Say, we have to be careful.
You just don't know what's there.
You've always got to move forwards.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, we copy an EMU check.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Just put your thing (UHT) in the holder, and move it over this way a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm not sure I can do that; but I'll give it a try.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (If) you make me knock my solar wind over, I'm going to be mad at you.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me give you a hand.
(Pause) Let me show you...You hold it there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Watching Al lift the seismometer) Hey, don't touch that (magnetometer).
I've got one Boyd bolt off it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Not sure of what Pete means) Want me to help you move it that way?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's got one Boyd bolt out of that magnetometer.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: All we're doing is making a mess.
You'd be better off...Let me...Listen, can you...Leave it; leave it sit.
You put that stool back here closer.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Am I over all the cables?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm trying to stay away from...That's a nice job on that solar wind.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You've got to be careful you don't kick dirt on them once we get them set down.
I guess the way you could do it is have it some sort of package and the package'd get all the dirt.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Well, I had to get this...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Here you go; move it up right here where my footprints are.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There; now, kind of tamp it right around in there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Positioning the PSE stool) Now, let's see what happens if we set it right...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That looks like it'd be okay, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me do a couple of things.
I take it and knock a little bit out of there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pull it over a little bit more and level it up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, this doesn't need to be level.
That's got kind of a deeper hole there in the center.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That doesn't have to level, huh?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: No, because the other one levels on this stool.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Push it down a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Just give me the tongs, and I'll put them away.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Get with it and I'll start opening
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's going to do okay, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Long Pause) If I wasn't at 3.7 psi, I'd whistle while I work.
(Four, short, monotonic, breathy attempts) (Garbled; Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That seismometer's right in position now.
Here's hoping...here's hoping nothing happens to it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It looks like it's going to clear.
Houston, looks like digging that little hole might work.
And it looks like maybe it's not going to get down in the hole.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We concur; it sounds like a good plan.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Well, I (should) put a little dirt on it, I guess.
When I put out the skirt of this foil, it doesn't want to lie down.
I guess because it's been folded so long.
I guess I can probably just spread it out.
(Pete chuckles; Pause) It acts almost like it gets a little static charge on it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's why (what) I want you to do is come take it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Take it out there and set it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) You might want to trip it off that thing, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Aligning the PSE or setting the magnetometer down) That shadow's going to be just right.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, everything is going great, but that whistle (on the comm circuit) is driving me nuts.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I can't help but get dirt on this darn thing, no matter how careful I am.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I don't think there's a way, Pete.
I don't think there's a way.

(Pause) Unless you got, you know, where you could put it out in a package and then your last step - like the SIDE over there - it just deploys off the thermal wrapping - or your dirt protector - and you end up with a nice, clean experiment sitting there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: How we doing on the timeline, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, it's just the way things (one of the
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, we show you're about 8 to 10 minutes behind but it's no sweat.
You've got lots of oxygen and feedwater.
We'll give you an update on the time remaining when you finish deploying ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Looks like it's going real good.
(On) this seismometer, I'm having a little trouble making the skirt lie down; but, other than that, it looks good.
It doesn't want to just lay flat like it does on Earth at one g.
It sort of wants to slip up.
I guess it's just because it's this memory in it from being folded so long, and...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You can make a two-man task out of that and put a little dirt around the outside edge to hold it down if you like.
(Pause) You won't have any trouble getting a little dirt on it, will you?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Houston, I noticed that if I just...
(Pause, hearing the second part of Gibson's remark) I'm pushing it down.
Let me level it (the PSE) up.
I think it's pretty near level.
(Pause) See if I can give it a couple of pushes.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I got this feeling there's a couple of
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: This thing looks level as can be to me.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll tell you an interesting thing about this bubble, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Slowly enunciating each word) I am not happy here, Al.
I'm afraid some of these Boyd bolts...There's not enough of them, and I don't understand why.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why, one of them didn't do or what?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I thought all of them did.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Looked like you're well ahead of the time.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, take your stick and kick a little dirt up on it right there (on the skirt).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Take your stick and just put a little dirt on the edge.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's okay; that skirt'll stay down enough.
Let me take a couple of pictures.
The passive seismic is down; the alignment is exactly 90 degrees.
And I'm going to take a couple pictures of it here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I don't know what's the matter, but something is...There it is.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey; look and see if the lens on my camera looks clean?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I don't know what's the matter.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Want me to hold down this part while you get that other bolt?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It sure wants to come up, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Central Station up and (confirming Al's report on the PSE alignment) 90 degrees on the PSE gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'll get you in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, we're going to have to do a lot of...
(Pause) What did you do, fall over?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I didn't even come close, actually.
It's just that I think it (his warning about moving backward)'s something you want to follow as a rule.
(Pause) But don't kick dirt on this (garbled) right there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled) One problem (is that) the Central Station is not really level.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (I'll) get a good shot of it here, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Central Station went up so nice.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, those are my last two
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Long Pause) I'm down in a little crater now, Houston.
And, sure enough, right in the bottom of the crater there is a lot softer dust than up on the rim.
I don't think the sides are slippery at all.
I don't think it's going to bother us going over to get our Surveyor.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Are you on your way out with the LSM (Lunar Surface Magnetometer)?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Got it right in hand; I'm out at the end of the line (50 ft SSE of the Central Station), and I'm deploying the legs right now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: One antenna mast in place, Houston.
Going back for my favorite thing.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Copy antenna mast in place and good luck.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Hey, one of the fun things here, Houston, is all these Styrofoam packing blocks that are put on there to protect it during shipment or launch.
When you take them off and throw them, they really sail.
These things stay airborne up for 10 seconds, maybe.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Got any more thoughts on our TV camera, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (They both laugh, probably as they watch another piece of Styrofoam sail off into the distance).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It hasn't hit the ground yet; it might have gone up 300 feet.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Laughing) Stop playing and get to work.
Maybe they'll extend us until 4-1/2 hours.
I feel like I could stay out here all day.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: This is going to be a good place for the magnetometer.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, we've been thinking on that (TV) camera, and when you get back (to the LM), we'll have a test for you to run on it.
It looks as though part of it's come back.
And we're going to try and see what we can salvage.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: What do you think happened to it, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, we're not sure on that.
Why don't you wait until you get back to the LM, and we'll work it out a bit and see if we can determine it?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (They both laugh) On Earth, they (magnetometer arms) just flop out in position.
Here they don't even want to stay.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Chuckling) That thing hit the ground and it's still bouncing.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pleased with how easily the magnetometer is deploying) That's slick.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, I was just thinking about something.
(Pause) Pete, no way to keep these things clean; I'm really worried about this white coating.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Lucky for me that this antenna head is almost (pause) level.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll see what I can do with it.
(The level bubble is) right in the center.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Dick, the EVA is going pretty well.
They're 2-1/2 hours into it; and they've got the ALSEP a good way deployed.
Apparently both of them look as though they just crawled out of a coal bin.
Other than that, things (are) pretty much nominal.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: The magnetometer's deployed; it's level; and it's pointed exactly east.
And the little black dot is right in the middle.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Same concern here (is that) there's just a lot of dust on top of the box where the electronics are.
Just hope it doesn't bother it too much.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yankee Clipper, we have a map update for Rev 19.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, Pete, let me take a couple of pictures of this.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What are you mumbling about over there?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, I just don't like all that dirt on it; don't know what we can do, though.
There's no way to work around it.
The radiator's got a little dirt on it, and there's no way to dust it off, there's no way to...
All you can do is tap it a little bit and hope some of it falls off and that's about it.
Don't want to tap it too hard.
(Pause) That's the best I can do.
(Long Pause while he takes the pictures)
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Okay; I'll deploy the SIDE, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's my last item (on the ALSEP portion of the checklist).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Does the antenna look like it's pointed at Earth?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Still getting the antenna aligned) Got it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I hope I don't tug on the (Central) station here (with the SIDE cable) as I come out, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It looks pretty level, by the way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm nearing the end (of the SIDE cable), I'll bet.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Laughing) They must have doubled the cable length on this one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me set it down and work on it right here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now what do I want to do?
(Long Pause as he consults his checklist)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Doesn't) want to do like that, does it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You've probably got them on you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: No, they weren't with that box.
We used them for something by your Central Station.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Ah, yeah, I got them on me.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, Houston, the antenna is up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: SIDE screen came out very nice.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Doesn't much want to stay down.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Commenting on the ease of antenna pointing) Okay.
That first (garbled); I can't believe it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, anytime you put anything spring-loaded on the Moon you're in trouble.
(Pete laughs) This screen has got sort of, you know, a spring to it?
Doesn't want to lie down much up here in this gravity.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, how long we been out?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, you've been out 2 plus 40, and the pacing one right now is Al (because he is not yet finished with the SIDE deployment).
And when you can, we'd like an EMU check, and if you'd give us your O2 readings?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And have you got anything for us?
Haven't heard too much from you.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It looks as though you're getting the job done.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: That little thing just doesn't want to...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, let me ask you a question.
Can I push in the shorting amps thing?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We understand you're not able to get a reading.
So, go ahead and close it, and we'll stand by for a zero reading.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Responding to the first part of Gibson's reply) All right.
(Responding to the second part) Okay, it might be full scale high.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, you'd better tell them I still haven't put the SIDE down.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That doesn't make any difference, does it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Does it make any difference, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're a big area litter bug.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, we'd like you to go ahead and get the full deployment done first before you depress the shorting amps.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (To Al) Boy, you're dirty all the way to your knees.
Can I give you any help?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And, Pete and Al, a comment on picture taking.
If you would, try to document some of the dirt which has gotten all over the equipment.
If you would, try to get close-ups which will show the dirt we might have on thermally-sensitive areas.
And, also, when you get done, if you would, take one or two extra pictures showing the ALSEP with the mounds that you described previously in the background.
That'll give us a good geometric reference.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I did a pan out here at the...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, let me hold this end and you stretch that end out.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Get the coils out of it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's got to head away from the Central Station; away from everything.
Probably ought to be a point right over there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But, now, I think if you kind of twirled it a couple times, it would end up uncoiling that twist in the line there.
Looks like it might be working.
Let me take and set this thing down, and it's supposed to point to the Earth this way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Get your ground wires wrapped around the legs.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Looks like it's going to be okay.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yankee Clipper, if you'll give us POO and ACCEPT, we'll ship you up a state vector, a target load, and a REFSMMAT.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Talking to the experiment package) Don't tip over.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah; wait a minute, wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Just hold it there just a...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Watch it, watch it, watch it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: No, your foot's hung in it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me sneak it back on again.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I want to put that (SIDE) lid back on there.
(But) it would probably be best if we just left it off.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think it would be best if you left it alone.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, you hold it there (either with his foot or a tool) while I (garbled)...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...because this (the CCIG he is manipulating) is going to want to turn it (the SIDE) over.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're coming up with a load.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Step on it (either the cable or the CCIG).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's what I think I can do.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Those guys got to be kidding about that cable on the...
I knew that thing was going to do that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Come on, this thing's making us run behind.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I know it, but I don't see any way to quickly get...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Laughing) All right; now, just a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me just try it a couple more times.
I think if I hold it this way and use this tool, I can probably set it...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, it's just like zero g; everything floats up.
That baby's going to nose dive into the dirt every time, sure as I'm standing here.
That cable's cleverly designed to make it do that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) There's no way (to get it properly oriented).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That thing goes right in the dirt each time...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Turn it around and point it the other way.
See if it'll point out that way.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I knew that ding-a-ling cable was going to make us spend hours trying to do that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Especially when you know it's going to happen.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Look, why can't I set it like this?
You just let go of the SIDE and come over and step on that thing.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, if this SIDE falls over, then I'm really going to be mad.
I got that thing firmly planted.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: If I can get this just arranged.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let go of the string in your hand.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's push it real hard in there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think we've got it licked.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (As the experiment falls over again) Aw, you...
The thing just jumps right up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, I'm just afraid that one's...
We've had it on this one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Laughing) You've had it on that one, my friend.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Look; I don't think it makes any difference if it lays on its side as long as it points its aperture in the right place.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's move it around here and just let it lay on its side.
(Pause) You know, it doesn't know the difference.
(Pause) Just kind of lay it around.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It looks like the only way...
This cable has just got too much spring for the weight of the package, and there's just no way.
Now, let me level it (the SIDE).
(Pause) No way (is the CCIG going to deploy properly).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) (To himself) What time is it, anyhow?
(Pause) (To Al) Let me get over here and get a big picture of this.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, you're 2 plus 48 into the EVA.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: That American flag sure looks pretty back here next to the LM, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think that's going to do it for the SIDE.
(Looking at his checklist to see what photos he needs to take).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Back off; I got the picture.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Can you see the bubble and everything in it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...I'm just getting one from a distance, here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, copy you have the SIDE deployed?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'm going to go get the shorting plug now, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, do I like to run up here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The first thing we've got to do is run over to that volcano-looking...
or whatever that little jobber-do is.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Houston, partially garbled under Gordon) How about Astro(naut) switch 1?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I need your help over here after a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're clear to rotate Astro switch 1, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We('ve) got to re-align that antenna.
I'd be a happy man if you tell me you're getting a signal.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: How about messing with this one thing right here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Also, I wanted to just look at the numbers (for the CS antenna pointing) with you, too.
Hey, just do me a favor.
Use your tongs and pick up a couple of these Boyd bolts and set them on the edge of that skirt on the passive seismic.
There's about 10 Boyd bolts just near it over there.
What did you want me to look at?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Just check that antenna; make sure it's level.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Look; you do that (Boyd bolt task).
I'm going to run over and photograph this ding-a-ling looking (mound)...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Looks like you did the job.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You just don't know how happy I am.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Tell me again the numbers you're supposed to use (for the antenna pointing).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: All right, I'm going over to this mound.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll be back in a flash.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll tell you the numbers I read; you tell me if they're right.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I don't know what this thing is.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I don't know what this (mound) is.
Let me get the (garbled) feet at f/8.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Houston, are you going to have a maneuver pad for me?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: We'll be up with you in about two minutes.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I think it's a little secondary impact crater.
Boy, is that a funny rock; it looks...(Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, here's a rock they'll be glad to see in Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It looks like a solid glass chunk.
Did you ever see any thing like it before?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Put this rock in your pack.
(Garbled) look at that, got that glass spatter on it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Never seen anything like that rock.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Are you through (with the deployment tasks)?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I got some pictures to take and that's it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled under Gordon) and I'll meet you over at that big mound.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Gibson) If you want to talk to me, you'll have to take it off of relay so that Pete and Al won't cut you out.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Going to bring the tongs, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You did not copy all of that PAD.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: It's impossible with those guys yakking.
transcript.speaker: Gordon
